plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-394 RP13.P02.M04.S07-RP13.P02.M06.S02 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-394 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP13.P02 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the sixteen remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first two rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H13), Claude review packet (C13), and CP00-395 handoff, without opening payments, payment matching, AR aging, or journal entry runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-394 is a descriptor-only closeout pack for the RP13 "Payments AR Accounting Export" program (risk B, 40 units, RP13.P02.M04.S07-RP13.P02.M06.S02), implementing the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (16 rows), the M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (22 rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (2 rows). I reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob across packages/payments/src (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js), the test (test/model.test.js), the validator (scripts/validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs), contracts/payments-core-contract.json, packages/payments/README.md, the closeout plan/baseline/manifest, and all review-hardening artifacts.

The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT overstated: production_ready is false in command-evidence.json, manifest.json (status "implemented_pending_review"), and the plan; only a descriptor-verification flag ("payments_core_p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified") and a coverage-based production_ready_candidate are asserted, with no enterprise-trust or runtime-ready claim. Every factory returns Object.freeze'd plain descriptors with no fs/network/DB/runtime side effects; the full no-write flag set is present and false (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, creates/updates_database_rows, reads/writes_object_storage, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, persists_idempotency_key, acquires_runtime_lock, etc.), and no_real_data/synthetic_only hold.

I independently recomputed the deliverable distribution (implementation 16, ui 6, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 4, test 8, contract 2, security_audit 2 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 16, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 2 = 40) from the baseline units; both match the prompt and registry requirements. Section row coverage (M04=16, M05=22, M06=2), row-key uniqueness, and descriptor-only row guards all check out. The contract's current_pack, p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, cp394_no_write_attestation, and historical_pack_bindings (ending CP00-394) match the registry and are deep-equality-asserted by the test and validator.

Authority boundary is intact across registry/service/validators/contract/manifest: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. Hermes appears only as a descriptor-only evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth-preflight passed with non-empty well-formed JSON; no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json/claude-review-result.json exist yet (correct pre-review state); invalid_review_attempts is empty; unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt json) are correctly segregated and not attributed to this pack. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3 observation about the intentionally cumulative no_write_attestation shape. Recommend PASS; pack and goal closeout are not blocked. Final human approval for any enterprise-trust claim remains required and outside this read-only review.

findings.title: CP394 no_write_attestation cumulatively retains upstream descriptor-validation flags
findings.details: PAYMENTS_CORE_CP394_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is constructed by spreading PAYMENTS_CORE_CP393_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (which spreads CP392's), so the CP394 attestation retains validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true and validates_p01_closeout_p02_foundation_descriptor_only:true alongside validates_p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_only:true (mirrored in contracts/payments-core-contract.json:5093-5115). This is intentional cumulative inheritance and is harmless: the CP394 descriptor factories re-execute the full upstream validation chain (createCp394 -> createCp393 -> createCp392), so the inherited flags are accurate, and the exact shape is deep-equality-checked by model.test.js and the rp13 validator. No write, boundary, real-data, or trust violation; noted only for transparency.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Optionally add a one-line note that the RP13 no_write_attestation is cumulative across the descriptor chain so future readers do not misread the inherited validates_*_descriptor_only flags as scope creep.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-394 payments core p02 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
